summary: Investigated cross-tab sync issue for user_a@theprogram.app. Root cause identified: LOG button in today.tsx SetRow is off-screen (x=446px in 375px viewport) due to flex layout overflow in web preview, making it inaccessible via normal taps. When LOG IS triggered (via JS event dispatch workaround), the full flow works: POST /api/log is called, backend saves the entry, and Log tab re-sync correctly reflects the updated counts. The [Today] and [Log] debug console.log statements were NOT captured by Playwright (appears to be an Expo web console interception issue).

ui_bugs.issue: LOG button overflows card bounds in web preview. getBoundingClientRect shows button at x=446 in a 375px viewport, with the row container only 309px wide (x=33). Caused by TextInput flex:1 items not respecting container width (missing minWidth:0). The card has overflow:hidden so the LOG button is visually clipped and inaccessible. On web preview, users CANNOT tap the LOG button.

[Today] Logging, [Today] Log result, [Today] Backend log failed, [Log] Re-sync console.log statements are NOT captured by Playwright page.on('console'). They may appear in browser DevTools but not in automated testing. This could be an Expo web console interception issue.

integration_issues.flow: Today tab LOG → Log tab re-sync
integration_issues.issue: Cross-tab sync logic IS correct when LOG is successfully triggered. Verified: POST /api/log sent to backend (backend logs confirm 2x POST /api/log), Log tab correctly shows updated count (11/14) and exercise states (GHR 3/3) after tab switch. The sync works end-to-end.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix LOG button overflow in today.tsx SetRow. Add minWidth:0 to sr.input (weight TextInput) and sr.repsInput styles so they properly shrink within the flex container. This will make the LOG button visible and tappable on web preview. Root cause: flex:1 TextInputs don't shrink below intrinsic content size (placeholder 'lbs' text) without minWidth:0, pushing LOG button to x=446 outside the 309px row container.
VERIFY: Test the LOG button fix on both web preview (375px viewport) and native. The LOG button should be within the card bounds and tappable.
LOW: Investigate why [Today]/[Log] debug console.log statements don't appear in Playwright console capture. May need to test via browser DevTools directly to see the actual log output.
critical_code_review_comments: today.tsx SetRow sr.input and sr.repsInput: Missing minWidth:0. In React Native web, flex:1 items need minWidth:0 to shrink below their natural content size. Without it, placeholder text prevents proper shrinking and LOG button overflows off-screen.

today.tsx handleLog: The console.log statements are correctly placed inside if(exerciseName) block. When LOG is triggered, the API call works and exercise names are correct (no fallback name issue found).

success_rate.backend: 100% - POST /api/log works, GET /api/log works, auth works
success_rate.frontend: 60% - Cross-tab sync logic correct but LOG button inaccessible on web due to layout overflow

seed_data_creation: Added test log entries for user_a via curl and JS dispatch during testing (GHR sets logged, count went from 9/14 to 11/14)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Cross-tab sync (Today→Log) is functionally correct. The bug is the LOG button overflow in today.tsx. Fix: add minWidth:0 to sr.input and sr.repsInput. After fix, test: login as user_a, tap LOG on 2 GHR sets in Today tab, switch to Log tab, verify GHR shows 2/3 logged.

rca_of_issue.bug: LOG button in today.tsx SetRow is off-screen (x=446px) in 375px viewport
rca_of_issue.root_cause: SetRow uses flexDirection:row with weight TextInput (flex:1) and repsInput (flex:1.3). These TextInputs don't have minWidth:0, so in web CSS, they use min-width:auto which prevents shrinking below intrinsic content size (placeholder text ~100px). This causes total row width to exceed the 309px container, pushing the LOG button (at the end of the row) to x=446, which is 104px outside the container. The ExerciseCard has overflow:hidden, visually clipping the button.
rca_of_issue.reproduction: 1. Login as user_a 2. Go to Today tab 3. Inspect LOG button position: x=446 in 375px viewport (confirmed via getBoundingClientRect) 4. User cannot tap LOG button on web preview
rca_of_issue.mitigation: Add minWidth:0 to sr.input and sr.repsInput in today.tsx SetRow StyleSheet. This forces TextInputs to properly shrink within flex container, keeping LOG button visible within card bounds.
rca_of_issue.sync_logic_status: Cross-tab sync logic WORKS CORRECTLY. When LOG is triggered: POST /api/log backend call succeeds, Log tab re-sync fetches updated logs, exercise logged states are correctly reflected.
